steps.title: Use the factor theorem
steps.content:
If $x + 2b$ is a factor of $3x^2 + ax + 14b$, then $x = -2b$ is a root. Substitute:
$$3(-2b)^2 + a(-2b) + 14b = 0$$
$$12b^2 - 2ab + 14b = 0$$
Divide by $2b$ ($b \neq 0$):
$$6b - a + 7 = 0 \implies a = 6b + 7$$

steps.title: Find b that's a positive integer
steps.content:
Test each choice's coefficient of $x$:
• (A) $a=7 \Rightarrow b=0$ — not positive.
• (B) $a=16 \Rightarrow b=1.5$ — not integer.
• (C) $a=18 \Rightarrow b=11/6$ — not integer.
• (D) $a=25 \Rightarrow b=3$ — positive integer ✓

That matches choice D — D is correct.
